notes: Default gravity deck for the BBS room. Online beats (a handle logs in, the printer jams), in-person beats (the alley door opens, the kettle boils), CB beats (channel 17 has chatter). The modem rack hums under every card.

deck.title: a handle dials in
deck.flavor: a handle you know logs in. their greeting line is shorter than usual tonight. their cursor sits at the prompt for a beat before they type.

deck.title: the printer jams
deck.flavor: the dot-matrix sticks on a long thread that came in at speed. the post in progress is half-printed. the back half is somewhere in the buffer.

deck.title: a floppy pops loose
deck.flavor: the drive ejects the floppy you weren't done with. you reinsert it. it reads cleanly. the eject was the drive's idea.

deck.title: the alley door opens
deck.flavor: somebody you didn't expect to see came in person. the alley door doesn't lock; it never has. the cicadas outside don't follow them in.

deck.title: the kettle clicks off
deck.flavor: the kettle is ready. somebody on the couch corner gets up to pour. the smell of hot sauce drifts over from the kitchen alcove.

deck.title: the ham band clears
deck.flavor: 14.230 has gone quiet. 14.301 has gone live. somebody is asking for sysop by callsign. sysop has not used that callsign in seven years.

deck.title: a long post comes in
deck.flavor: two thousand words land in your inbox at once. they are not bad. they are not finished. they are somebody trying to figure something out by typing it.

deck.title: the modem chirps three times
deck.flavor: three handshakes in a row. the BBS is doing the thing it does when the night settles into it.

deck.title: the phone line drops
deck.flavor: modem 2 disconnects. somebody's mid-post. the BBS holds the partial draft in case they reconnect within ninety seconds. they will, in seventy.

deck.title: the lurker pauses
deck.flavor: the lurker's cursor blinks at the bottom of the reply box. they have been about to post for ten minutes. they have typed and erased the same first sentence four times.

deck.title: the signal settles
deck.flavor: the room is humming. you can hear the modem rack singing — a thin tone at the edge of audible that sysop has been able to hear for nine years.

deck.title: the archive serves up
deck.flavor: you reach for a floppy and it's the right floppy. the floppy wall does this sometimes; sysop has stopped explaining it to people who notice.

deck.title: a new handle
deck.flavor: a registration request from a handle you've never seen. the handle has a familiarity to it though, the way some new handles do. you approve it.

deck.title: the BBS goes quiet
deck.flavor: all four modems on standby for five minutes straight. the printer is silent. the cicadas outside fill the room the way they fill a room when nothing else is.

deck.title: the signal holds
deck.flavor: every modem is still up. every thread is still active. for thirty seconds the BBS is what it should always be — small, awake, holding the room together for the people who chose to be in it.
